name: Bob Crosby
abstract: George Robert Crosby (August 23, 1913 – March 9, 1993) was an American jazz singer and bandleader, best known for his group the Bob-Cats, which formed around 1935. The Bob-Cats were a New Orleans Dixieland-style jazz octet.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Salute to Eddie Condon
Newport Jazz Festival: The Stardust Road: A Hoagy Carmichael Jubilee

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Alley
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee explicitly corrects the interviewer, stating 'Bill Cosby' and not 'Bob Crosby'. There is no mention of Bob Crosby in the interviewee's narrative.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Alley interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Conniff
about_quotes.evidence: Then I did the same thing with the Bob Crosby band in 1939... I wrote for Bob Crosby and the guys in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Conniff interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Pell
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Bob Crosby out there... I took Eddie Miller’s place on Bob Crosby’s band
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Pell interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 11, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: I did a thing that Bob Crosby...da da da da da-da-da (humming)...that's "Cow Cow Blues," right? ... Bob Crosby took the whole arrangement, note for note, and didn't say one word to me.
